'''David Tangata-Toa''' (born 15 July 1981) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer and current assistant coach for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL). Primarily a {{rlp|pr}}, he played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and [[Celtic Crusaders]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Born on the [[Gold Coast, Queensland]], Tangata-Toa played junior rugby league for the [[Burleigh Bears]] and attended [[Palm Beach Currumbin State High School]].

==Playing career==
A lower grader with the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]] and [[Cronulla Sharks]], Tangata-Toa moved to [[England]] in 2005, joining [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|National League One]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.hullkr.co.uk/on-this-day-24-04/|title=On This Day: 24.04|website=Hull Kingston Roverd}}</ref> In 2006, he played in their National League One Grand Final winning side, helping them earn promotion to the [[Super League]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2006/oct/09/rugbyleague.sport|title=Rugby League: National League One grand final|date=8 October 2006|website=The Guardian}}</ref> In 2007, he played 17 Super League games for the club, scoring three tries.

In 2008, Tangata-Toa joined the [[Celtic Crusaders]], scoring a try in their [[RFL Championship|National League One]] Grand Final loss to the [[Salford City Reds]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7638206.stm|title=Celtic Crusaders 18-36 Salford|date=28 September 2008|publisher=BBC Sport}}</ref> In 2009, he played 19 Super League games for the Crusaders, scoring four tries.

==Coaching career==
In 2011, Tangata-Toa became [[player-coach|captain-coach]] of the [[Windsor Wolves]] in the [[Bundaberg Red Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://leagueunlimited.com/news/nswrl-bundaberg-red-cup-teams-round-10|title=NSWRL Bundaberg Red Cup Teams – Round 10|date=11 May 2011|website=League Unlimited}}</ref> In 2012, he moved up to Windsor's [[NSW Cup]] side as an assistant coach.

In 2013, Tangata-Toa joined the [[Penrith Panthers]] as an assistant coach to their [[NRL Under-20s|under-20]] side. In Round 7, Tangata-Toa replaced [[Garth Brennan]], who moved up to the club's NSW Cup side, as the head coach.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/newslocal/west/penrith-panthers-sign-st-george-illawarra-dragons-five-eighth-jamie-soward-on-a-four-year-deal/story-fngr8j33-1226623620464|title=Daily Telegraph}}</ref> Brennan later returned to the role before the finals series began.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/cleary-backs-brennan-as-knights-coach/tabid/10874/newsid/80437/default.aspx|title=Cleary backs Brennan as Knights coach|publisher=National Rugby League|accessdate=18 October 2017}}</ref> From 2014 to 2016, he worked as an assistant coach to the Panthers' NSW Cup side. In 2016, he became the assistant coach of the [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tongan national team]] under head coach [[Kristian Woolf]].<ref name=tonga>{{cite news|last=Logue|first=Matt|date=19 April 2016|title=Andrew Johns has selflessly offered his services to help mentor the Tongan national team|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/andrew-johns-has-selflessly-offered-his-services-to-help-mentor-the-tongan-national-team/news-story/0ab15598cfe8f05a7392a8ce9c806bab|newspaper=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|accessdate=20 March 2017}}</ref>

In 2017, he once again took charge of Penrith's under-20 side, leading them to the finals.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.penrithpanthers.com.au/news/2017/08/05/updated-nyc-teamlist-round-22/|title=Updated NYC Teamlist: Round 22|date=5 August 2017|website=Penrith Panthers}}</ref>

In 2018, Tangata-Toa became head coach of the [[Townsville Blackhawks]] [[Hastings Deering Colts|under-20]] side, where he led the side to the minor premiership and Grand Final.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2017/10/05/blackhawks-name-u18-u20-coaches/|title=Blackhawks name U18, U20 coaches|date=5 October 2017|website=QRL}}</ref> In 2019, he joined the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] as an assistant coach.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2018/09/27/tangata-toa-joins-coaching-staff/|title=Tangata-Toa to join Cowboys coaching staff|date=27 September 2018|website=North Queensland Cowboys}}</ref>

On 21 September, Tangata-Toa joined the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] as a transition coach after two years with the Cowboys.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bulldogs.com.au/news/2020/09/21/craig-sandercock-and-david-tangata-toa-to-complete-bulldogs-coaching-line-up-for-2021/|title=Craig Sandercock and David Tangata-Toa to complete Bulldogs coaching line-up for 2021|date=21 September 2020|website=Canterbury Bulldogs}}</ref>

==Personal life==
Tangata-Toa's older brother, [[Andrew Tangata-Toa|Andrew]], is a former professional rugby league player.<ref name=tonga />
